data.title: PulteGroup earns 2025 Great Place To Work certification for the 7th time with 93% of employees saying it's great.
data.description: PulteGroup, Inc. has been recognized as a Great Place To Work® for the seventh time. The company, listed on NYSE as PHM and ranked as the third-largest homebuilding entity in America, received the certification based on feedback from employees. An impressive 93% of PulteGroup employees expressed that it is indeed a Great Place To Work, in stark contrast to the 57% average in typical U.S. companies. This recognition underscores the company's commitment to providing a positive and fulfilling work environment for its workforce.

data.title: Global M2M/IoT market grows by 14% in 2024, reaching 3.8 billion subscribers by the year-end, making up 30% of all mobile subscribers. Subscribers expected to grow at 11.0% CAGR until 2029 - ResearchAndMarkets.com.
data.description: A newly added report on the Global M2M/IoT Communications Market reveals that the global number of cellular IoT subscribers surged by 14% in 2024, reaching 3.8 billion by the year's end. This figure represents approximately 30% of all mobile subscribers worldwide. Projections indicate that by 2029, the number of cellular IoT subscribers is anticipated to continue expanding at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 11.0%.

data.title: Decentralized AI disrupted by Blockchain AI as Web3 projects struggle with forced integration, hindering innovation.
data.description: AI projects leveraging Web3 technology frequently require integration with blockchain to secure funding, leading to a detrimental impact on innovation as the focus shifts towards ecosystem compatibility rather than effective AI solutions.

data.title: Macerich to report EPS of $0.09; past performance saw 2.84% drop; stock trading at $16.58, up 15.06% in 52-weeks. Analysts rate as Buy with $20.0 target, 20.63% potential upside.
data.description: Macerich (NYSE: MAC) will reveal its quarterly earnings on 2025-08-11, with analysts anticipating an EPS of $0.09. Investors hope for results surpassing estimates and optimistic guidance for the next quarter. Previous earnings saw a $0.02 EPS beat resulting in a 2.84% share price drop. In the past quarters, Macerich's EPS estimates varied, with actual results leading to price changes. As of August 07, Macerich shares traded at $16.58, showing a 15.06% increase over 52 weeks. Analysts hold a consensus Buy rating on Macerich, with an average one-year price target of $20.0, suggesting a 20.63% upside potential. Understanding market sentiments and expectations is crucial for investors.

data.title: JFrog reports Q2 earnings of 18 cents/share, beats sales estimate with $127.22 million revenue. Raises FY25 guidance to $507-510 million. CEO optimistic about growth.
data.description: JFrog Ltd (NASDAQ: FROG) announced strong second-quarter financial results, surpassing expectations. The company reported quarterly earnings of 18 cents per share, exceeding the anticipated 16 cents. Their quarterly sales also outperformed, reaching $127.22 million compared to the expected $122.75 million. Moreover, JFrog raised its FY2025 sales guidance from $500 million-$5050 million to $507 million-$510 million. CEO Shlomi Ben Haim highlighted the company's strategic focus on DevOps, Security, and MLOps, establishing itself as a pivotal system for all software packages. Additionally, they are recognized as a frontrunner in the rapidly expanding AI sector as a gold-standard model registry.

data.title: Track Group reported Q3 FY25 financial results with total revenue of $9.1M (1% decrease), $4.6M gross profit (8% increase), $0.8M operating income (244% increase), and net income of $1.4M (compared to $0.9M net loss in Q3 FY24). Adjusted EBITDA for Q3 FY25 was $1.8M. Cash balance at June 30, 2025, was $4.9M, a 37% increase from September 30, 2024. Business outlook shows expected revenue of $34.5-35.5M for FY25 with adjusted EBITDA margin of 14.0-18.0%. Track Group focuses on offender tracking & monitoring services.
data.description: Track Group, Inc. reported financial results showing a total revenue of $9.1 million in Q3 FY25, a decrease of 1% from $9.2 million in Q3 FY24. The gross profit increased by 8% to $4.6 million in Q3 FY25 compared to $4.3 million in Q3 FY24. Operating income was $0.8 million in Q3 FY25 compared to an operating loss of ($0.5 million) in Q3 FY24. The company had a cash balance of $4.9 million at June 30, 2025, up 37% from $3.6 million at September 30, 2024. Net income attributable to shareholders was $1.4 million in Q3 FY25, a significant improvement from a net loss of ($0.9 million) in Q3 FY24. Adjusted EBITDA for Q3 FY25 was $1.8 million compared to $1.6 million for Q3 FY24. The company is optimistic about continued growth and expects revenue between $34.5-35.5 million for FY25 with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 14.0-18.0%. Track Group specializes in offender tracking and monitoring services, aiming to support re-socialization and monitoring initiatives in the industry. They are listed on the OTCQB exchange under the ticker symbol "TRCK." Prioritizing cost control and strategic focus has contributed to their favorable financial performance and outlook for sustainable growth.

data.title: Crypto firm Algoz hits $100M AUM in H1 2025, managing 50+ SMAs. Specializes in algorithmic trading for digital assets.
data.description: On Friday, Algoz, a quantitative asset manager focusing on crypto, announced reaching a milestone of exceeding $100 million in assets under management (AUM) in the first half of 2025. Currently, the company is actively overseeing more than 50 self-managed accounts (SMAs), indicating a notable advancement in crypto quantitative investing. Established in 2017, Algoz concentrates on employing algorithmic trading tactics for digital assets. The firm experienced rapid expansion in early 2024 after introducing Quant Pro, a settlement platform designed for off-exchange transactions to mitigate counterparty risk. Additionally, in the same year, Algoz integrated with...

data.title: New Klecknersville Substation powers 5,000+ residents and businesses, boosting reliability in Northampton County.
data.description: In Reading, Pennsylvania, as of August 8, 2025, over 5,000 individuals and companies located in the rapidly expanding Northampton County are currently benefiting from a recently activated substation. This infrastructure, known as the Klecknersville Substation, has been constructed by FirstEnergy to bolster the area's progression.

data.title: Symmons Industries chosen as finalist in 2025 SuperNova Awards for tech leadership in Data to Decisions category.
data.description: Symmons Industries, based in Braintree, Massachusetts, has been honored as a finalist in the Data to Decisions category for the prestigious Constellation SuperNova Awards. The awards, now in their 15th year, commend individuals and groups who are at the forefront of driving digital transformation. The recognition highlights the company's disruptive technology leadership. The winners will be announced on August 8, 2025.

data.title: Cencosud's revenue grows by 5.3% in Q2 2025 to CLP $4,171,343 million (USD 4,405 million), boosted by Chile, Argentina, Peru, and the US.
data.description: In Santiago, Chile, Cencosud S.A. released its second-quarter 2025 results, revealing revenues of CLP $4,171,343 million (USD 4,405 million). This marks a 5.3% growth from the previous year. Excluding the hyperinflation impact in Argentina, revenue surged by 7.2%. The growth stemmed from increased revenues in all business units in Chile and Argentina. Additionally, Colombia experienced a significant upturn, registering its most substantial sales growth since 2022.

data.title: Gilead Sciences beats earnings with $2.01 per share, $7.08 billion sales in Q2 2025, lifting annual outlook.
data.description: Gilead Sciences reported its second-quarter 2025 earnings, with adjusted earnings per share at $2.01, exceeding the consensus of $1.94. Sales for the quarter reached $7.08 billion, surpassing the consensus of $6.94 billion. Total product sales in Q2 2025 increased by 2%, with non-Veklury sales up by 4% to $6.9 billion due to higher HIV, Livdelzi, and Trodelvy sales, offset by lower chronic HCV sales. Biktarvy sales rose by 9% to $3.5 billion, Descovy sales increased by 35% to $653 million, while Liver Disease portfolio sales decreased by 4% to $795 million. Veklury sales dropped by 44% to $121 million, Cell Therapy sales fell by 7% to $485 million. Trodelvy sales spiked by 14% to $364 million. In Q2 2025, product gross margin stood at 78.7%, up from 77.7% in the same period last year. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Crypto's Green Dildo Coin backers orchestrated WNBA dildo-throwing stunts as part of a humorous marketing campaign.
data.description: Supporters of Green Dildo Coin have stated that the recent series of dildo-throwing incidents at WNBA games are a deliberate and humorous part of a coordinated marketing strategy to promote their coin. The campaign involving the provocative and attention-grabbing stunts aims to generate buzz and draw attention to the cryptocurrency. The backers believe that these actions will help increase visibility and interest in Green Dildo Coin among potential investors. The explicit and unconventional approach has garnered significant media coverage and sparked discussions within the community, with the viral campaign attracting widespread attention online.

data.title: Law firm Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP investigates investor claims on Sarepta Therapeutics stock between June 22, 2023 and June 24, 2025. Contact to discuss potential losses.
data.description: Securities Litigation Partner James (Josh) Wilson from Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is urging investors who experienced losses in Sarepta to reach out directly to him for a discussion on their potential options. If you bought or gained securities in Sarepta within the period of June 22, 2023, and June 24, 2025, and are interested in exploring further, you are encouraged to contact Wilson to consider your next steps.

data.title: Saks Global completes $600M financing, receives $300M in Exchange Offer. 98% of old notes tendered.
data.description: On August 8, 2025, a company successfully raised $300 million through an Exchange Offer. Approximately 98% of the total outstanding Old Notes, amounting to a significant aggregate principal sum, have been tendered in the Exchange Offer. Additionally, holders have provided their consents to the proposed amendments by the Company.

data.title: New Rivermont Lynchburg Leesville campus to open in August 2025, inviting community members, families, and partners.
data.description: A new purpose-built school in Lynchburg, Virginia, is set to welcome students in August 2025. Rivermont Schools is inviting community members, families, and district partners to the opening of the new Rivermont Lynchburg Leesville campus on August 20, 2025. This event marks the beginning of a new chapter in education, offering enhanced learning opportunities for students. This purpose-built school demonstrates a commitment to providing quality education and facilities for the community. The opening ceremony will be a significant milestone in education, showcasing a state-of-the-art facility that will accommodate a significant number of students.
